entries.falsifiableTrigger: NVIDIA rpoBacklog (multi-year cloud commitments) and its $119B supply commitments grow <10% while Data Center revenue keeps rising, for 2 consecutive quarters.
entries.mechanism: Hyperscaler AI capex ($660-690B, heading toward $3-4T) exceeds NVIDIA's packaging/HBM-limited output, so backlog, supply commitments and >12-month lead times accumulate faster than shipments.
entries.sensitivity: Hyperscaler capex trajectory and how much custom-ASIC in-housing diverts incremental demand.
entries.statement: Datacenter GPU demand continues to outrun NVIDIA's ability to serve it.
entries.title: NVDA demand durability

entries.falsifiableTrigger: CoWoS-S/L leadTimes fall below 26 weeks AND HBM3E returns to available (not sold-out) supply for 2 consecutive quarters while demand indicators stay positive.
entries.mechanism: Advanced 2.5D packaging (CoWoS) and HBM stacks are the physical chokepoints for accelerator assembly; with both fully booked, shippable units are capped regardless of wafer starts or order demand.
entries.sensitivity: Pace of CoWoS capacity expansion and incremental HBM3E/12-hi supply coming online.
entries.statement: Advanced packaging + HBM — not wafers or demand — cap realizable merchant-GPU revenue.
entries.title: Supply constraint binding

entries.falsifiableTrigger: AMD Data Center revenue growth falls below 20% YoY with no new named hyperscaler MI-series deployment for 2 consecutive quarters, or Instinct accelerator share stalls below 6%.
entries.mechanism: Hyperscalers seeking supply diversification and lower cost adopt Instinct (Meta 6GW), converting AMD's roadmap into recurring datacenter revenue and share.
entries.sensitivity: On-schedule MI450 ramp in 2H 2026 and AMD's HBM/CoWoS allocation.
entries.statement: AMD converts its Instinct roadmap into material datacenter share.
entries.title: AMD credible second source

entries.falsifiableTrigger: NVIDIA hyperscale/Data Center revenue growth reaccelerates with no new scaled custom-ASIC deployment for 2 consecutive quarters, or a named ASIC program (Ironwood, Trainium 3, MI450-custom) is cancelled or slips ≥12 months.
entries.mechanism: Hyperscalers with concentrated inference/training workloads build cost-optimized ASICs (Ironwood, Trainium), capturing incremental AI compute that would otherwise be merchant GPUs.
entries.sensitivity: Relative $/token cost of ASICs vs GPUs and hyperscaler tolerance of software-ecosystem switching costs.
entries.statement: Hyperscaler custom silicon meaningfully displaces merchant-GPU demand growth.
entries.title: Custom ASIC substitution

entries.falsifiableTrigger: D6 B200/H100 median $/GPU-hour declines ≥15% at ≥2 tracked providers within one quarter, or H100 median falls below $2.50/GPU-hr for 2 consecutive quarters.
entries.mechanism: Packaging/HBM-constrained rollout keeps rental capacity behind demand, sustaining high utilization and firm clearing prices.
entries.sensitivity: Pace of new datacenter capacity online versus inference demand, and older-gen (H100/A100) erosion as B200/GB200 ramp.
entries.statement: GPU rental/spot price levels hold up despite added supply.
entries.title: Pricing power persistence

entries.falsifiableTrigger: Commerce removes H200/advanced-compute China case-by-case licensing and the 25% Section-232 tariff, AND NVIDIA books >$2B China Data Center compute revenue for 2 consecutive quarters.
entries.mechanism: Export licensing and tariffs foreclose China compute demand, but overwhelming supply-limited non-China demand absorbs capacity, so controls trim addressable market without constraining realized revenue.
entries.sensitivity: US-China policy direction and whether non-China demand stays supply-limited enough to mask any China loss.
entries.statement: Export-control policy materially constrains addressable merchant-GPU demand.
entries.title: Export control exposure

entries.falsifiableTrigger: NVIDIA's 10-Q discloses customer financing/notes receivable rising for 2 consecutive quarters, or a named financed AI-cloud/neocloud customer defaults or fails to convert a financed commitment to cash revenue within 1 quarter.
entries.mechanism: If NVIDIA underwrote purchases via credit or revenue-share, reported demand would partly reflect NVIDIA-funded orders, creating a loop exposed to customer default — but observed demand is customer-capex-funded.
entries.sensitivity: Disclosure of financing receivables and the financial health of concentrated neocloud/hyperscaler customers.
entries.statement: NVIDIA's own revenue-sharing and credit-support financing increasingly underwrites its accelerator demand, coupling reported demand strength to NVIDIA-extended credit and adding circularity risk to buildout durability.
entries.title: Vendor-financed demand circularity

entries.falsifiableTrigger: NVIDIA top-3 direct-customer concentration falls below 40% of total revenue (from 54%) for 2 consecutive quarters per 10-Q customerConcentration disclosure.
entries.mechanism: With over half of revenue from three buyers, any one pausing capex or in-housing to ASICs materially dents NVIDIA revenue independent of aggregate AI demand.
entries.sensitivity: Hyperscaler capex cyclicality and in-housing/ASIC decisions by the top-3 accounts.
entries.statement: NVIDIA's data-center revenue is increasingly concentrated in a few direct customers, raising demand-durability risk if any single hyperscaler slows orders or shifts to in-house silicon.
entries.title: Customer concentration risk

entries.falsifiableTrigger: NVIDIA networking revenue grows slower than Data Center compute revenue for 2 consecutive quarters, or falls below 15% of Data Center revenue.
entries.mechanism: Selling rack-scale fabric (GB200 NVL72, DGX Rubin) locks customers into NVIDIA's system architecture, raising the cost and complexity of substituting merchant or ASIC compute using non-NVIDIA interconnect.
entries.sensitivity: Adoption of open interconnect standards (UALink/Ultra Ethernet) and ASIC clusters built on non-NVIDIA fabric.
entries.statement: NVIDIA's rack-scale networking is scaling faster than its compute, deepening a full-system moat that raises switching costs versus merchant accelerators and custom ASICs.
entries.title: Networking attach as systems moat
